summary: Iteration 14 — XLM (Stellar Testnet) integration, backend-only per review. 15/15 pytest PASS. New /api/wallet/xlm/info + /api/wallet/xlm/send routes behave per spec: (a) fresh /api/auth/register auto-derives an XLM address (starts 'G', exactly 56 chars) exposed via /wallet/assets `xlm_address` + XLM asset entry with on_chain:true, network='Testnet', wallet_address matching top-level. (b) /wallet/xlm/info returns all required keys: address, balance_stroops, balance_xlm, network=Testnet, explorer (stellar.expert), faucet=https://friendbot.stellar.org/?addr=<addr>, send_supported:true, min_reserve_xlm:1.0. (c) POST /wallet/xlm/send with a valid 56-char G... recipient but an unfunded source correctly returns HTTP 400 with detail 'Insufficient XLM (need ...+1 reserve, have 0.0000). Stellar requires a 1 XLM minimum reserve to keep accounts active.' — clean JSON, no 500, no traceback. (d) Invalid recipient 'foo' → 400 'Invalid Stellar (G...) address'. (e) Negative + zero amount → 422 (Pydantic Field(gt=0)). (f) Missing token → 401. (g) Regressions green: /wallet/eth/send + /wallet/usdc/send + /wallet/btc/send + /wallet/sol/send all still 400 on invalid addresses; smoketest@vaulted.app login works; /api/ health = ok. (h) Confirmed via backend logs: no ERROR / traceback lines during test run. Note on review brief: the provided sample recipient `GCEAKB44V4S7YE4CTPZKVTZFYCRYJHVL7O53J7RUWRMTGSAOCD3AYN` is only 54 chars (truncated) — backend correctly rejects it as 'Invalid Stellar (G...) address' at server.py:902 before pre-flight balance check. Substituted a real 56-char G... address in the test to actually exercise the unfunded-source path. Frontend code-read: /app/frontend/app/send.tsx correctly routes asset==='XLM' → POST /wallet/xlm/send with body {to_address, amount, memo}; /app/frontend/app/receive.tsx has XLM in Asset union type + CHAIN_LABEL/CHAIN_PATH/CHAIN_BADGE maps — wiring matches backend schema exactly.

minor.issue: Pre-flight insufficient-XLM check swallows Horizon read errors (`except Exception: stroops = None`) — if Horizon is down we skip the friendly 400 and reach stellar_sdk which then raises RuntimeError('XLM account not funded yet…') caught by the generic `except Exception` at server.py:928 returning 400 'XLM submission failed: XLM account not funded yet…'. Behaviour is still correct (400, no traceback), just worth noting the error text is slightly different in that failure mode.

server.py now well past 2000 lines; XLM routes cleanly fit alongside BTC/SOL/USDC — a routers/wallet_multichain.py extract is overdue. Also carry-over from iter13: 28-byte JWT_SECRET, bcrypt __about__ AttributeError, expo doctor package drift.

ui_bugs.issue: Line 217 uses `selected.address` but the backend /wallet/assets payload returns the field as `wallet_address` (see server.py:499) and the local `Asset` type (send.tsx:15-18) declares neither. Result: `encodeURIComponent(selected.address || '')` produces `https://friendbot.stellar.org/?addr=` (empty addr param), so Friendbot rejects the funding request. Fix: `selected.wallet_address` and extend the Asset type with `wallet_address?: string`.

action_items: (Frontend, MEDIUM) In send.tsx line 217, change `selected.address` → `selected.wallet_address` and extend the local `Asset` type with `wallet_address?: string;` so the XLM Friendbot faucet button actually funds the correct account (`?addr=<G...>`).

(Info only) The recipient address in the review brief (`GCEAKB44V4S7YE4CTPZKVTZFYCRYJHVL7O53J7RUWRMTGSAOCD3AYN`) is 54 chars — the backend's length gate at server.py:902 correctly rejects it. If E1 wants to hand-test with curl, use a real 56-char address (e.g. `GDQP2KPQGKIHYJGXNUIYOMHARUARCA7DJT5FO2FFOOKY3B2WSQHG4W37`).
(Carry-over from iter13) Split server.py into routers/; upgrade JWT_SECRET to 32+ bytes; pin bcrypt==4.0.1; run `npx expo install --fix` to align expo/expo-font/expo-router with SDK 54.
critical_code_review_comments: xlm_send_route (server.py:894-951) is symmetric with sol_send_route: auto-derive addresses first, syntactic G... validation, pre-flight balance check via fetch_xlm_balance_stroops (with 1.0001 XLM reserve buffer covering both base reserve + fee headroom), typed ValueError vs generic Exception split so the 400 detail carries stellar_sdk's readable message, tx record persisted only after successful submit — solid.

multichain.xlm_send (multichain.py:301-349) uses stellar_sdk.Server.load_account to detect unfunded source (Horizon 404 → RuntimeError with actionable message), builds via TransactionBuilder with add_time_bounds(0,0), amount formatted to 7 decimals (Stellar canonical), memo truncated to 28 bytes — all per SEP-005/006. Base fee 100 stroops is well below current network minimum.

derive_addresses (multichain.py:65-90) now returns btc + sol + xlm using bip_utils Bip44Coins.STELLAR (SLIP-10 ed25519, m/44'/148'/0'). Signing uses stellar_sdk.Keypair.from_raw_ed25519_seed(bip_utils raw private) — matches the SEP-005 test vectors.
explorer_url_xlm (multichain.py:259-261) uses stellar.expert /explorer/testnet/account/... which is correct for testnet. For mainnet it switches to /explorer/public — good.
SendXlmIn model (server.py:888-891) has amount: float = Field(gt=0) giving free 422 on zero/negative; memo: Optional[str] is stripped to 28 bytes downstream in xlm_send.
One-line polish opportunity: server.py:882 concatenates the faucet URL with `+ a`; using an f-string would be more consistent with the rest of the file. Non-blocking.
(Carry-over) server.py at ~2050 lines; wallet_*_send + wallet_*_info routes are the natural sub-module.

success_rate.frontend: not tested (backend-only per review_request); code-read confirms wiring is correct except for one MEDIUM bug on the Friendbot faucet link

seed_data_creation: Two fresh TEST_iter14_<uuid>@vaulted.app users per pytest session via /api/auth/register (one module-scoped for auth headers, one for the register→assets flow). smoketest@vaulted.app reused read-only. Zero on-chain funds moved — fresh Stellar accounts are unfunded by design (hits the intended pre-flight 400).
context_for_next_testing_agent: XLM backend is production-ready modulo the frontend Friendbot faucet URL bug (send.tsx:217 — uses `selected.address` instead of `selected.wallet_address`, so the ?addr= param ends up empty and Friendbot can't fund the account). Sample 56-char testnet G-address for hand-testing curl: `GDQP2KPQGKIHYJGXNUIYOMHARUARCA7DJT5FO2FFOOKY3B2WSQHG4W37`. Note the review-brief address is 54 chars and is (correctly) rejected by the length gate.
rca_of_issue: No backend bug found. The initial pytest fail was self-inflicted — the sample recipient copied from the review brief is 54 chars, so it hit the 'Invalid Stellar (G...) address' 400 at server.py:902 before the pre-flight balance check. Swapping to a real 56-char G-address exercises the intended unfunded-source path and returns the expected 'Insufficient XLM (need 1.0+1 reserve, have 0.0000). Stellar requires a 1 XLM minimum reserve...' 400.
